hypothesis: Systems-optimizer salvage becomes safer if runner-policy must preserve the exact seven singleton command groups for the scope-local promotion bundle and trailing observability commands, because flatten-only verification lets merged groups weaken required proof steps into alternatives.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` flattened `runner-policy.json.salvage.required_command_groups_by_lane['systems-optimizer']`, so a malformed salvage policy could merge `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` and `node CLAW/scripts/verify-ggd-binding.mjs` into one alternative group and still verify clean. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any systems-optimizer salvage plan whose seven required groups are not singleton and exact-order, while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if systems-optimizer salvage must expose the exact seven singleton command groups, flatten-equivalent merged groups are rejected, and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Systems-optimizer salvage commands must retain exact singleton group boundaries; flatten-equivalent merged groups weaken required proofs into alternatives.
